commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `telecrawl import --chat ID`, threads it into the Telegram Desktop importer, skips broad dialog/folder enumeration for that mode, and upserts only the selected chat's archive rows.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction. Current-main source confirms there is no `import --chat` mode, and the PR body provides redacted runtime output for the proposed new mode.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed from this review; maintainers should make the product/API call on the new CLI flag and decide whether regression coverage is required before merge.

target.title: feat: add --chat flag to import for single-chat imports
target.body:
Adds a `--chat` flag to `telecrawl import` that targets a single chat/group/channel by its JID (e.g. `-1001749683370`).

When `--chat` is set:
- Resolves the entity directly by ID instead of listing all recent dialogs
- Skips global folder enumeration (`load_folders`) to avoid broad dialog scans
- Uses `UpsertChat` instead of `ReplaceAll` — only replaces the target chat's rows, preserving all other imported data in the archive
- Fetches only that chat's messages, making large-group imports practical

## Proof (redacted runtime output)

```
$ telecrawl import --chat -1001749683370 --messages-limit 100000
source_path: /Users/.../Library/Application Support/Telegram Desktop/tdata
db_path: /Users/.../.telecrawl/telecrawl.db
chats: 1
messages: 82925
media_messages: 6393
started_at: 2026-05-24T04:39:04Z
finished_at: 2026-05-24T04:52:57Z
```

82,925 messages imported from a single group in ~14 minutes, pulling history back to Feb 2022. Existing archive data from other chats is preserved.

Changes: 3 files, +67/-3
target.author: nullyn
